lead_signal.headline: No new FIMI operations identified this week — exhaustive Tier 1-3 search returned no disclosures dated May 9-15, 2026
lead_signal.body: Exhaustive search across Meta CIB, Google TAG, Microsoft MSTIC, EU EEAS, Stanford IO, DFRLab, ASPI, Bellingcat, and EU DisinfoLab returned no new FIMI-specific disclosures, platform takedowns, government attributions, or institutional publications dated within the May 9-15, 2026 window. Most recent actionable Tier 1 material remains Meta H1 2026 Adversarial Threat Report (March 11, 2026) and Google TAG Q4 2025 Bulletin (January 29, 2026). No new TAG bulletin for Q1 2026 was found. This is a null-signal week reflecting disclosure cadence gaps rather than operational cessation.

module_0.body: Exhaustive search across Meta CIB, Google TAG, Microsoft MSTIC, EU EEAS, Stanford IO, DFRLab, ASPI, Bellingcat, and EU DisinfoLab returned no new FIMI-specific disclosures, platform takedowns, government attributions, or institutional publications dated within the May 9-15, 2026 window. This is a null-signal week reflecting disclosure cadence gaps rather than operational cessation.

campaigns.summary: Meta H1 2026 report (March 11, 2026) updated attribution for persistent Iranian influence operation Endless Mayfly to Iran International Union of Virtual Media (IUVM). A separate Iranian operation targeting English-speaking audiences was disrupted in Q4 2025 before building authentic audience. No new activity documented in May 9-15 window.
campaigns.asymmetric: IUVM attribution represents rare platform-level technical attribution to named Iranian state entity; demonstrates increasing platform willingness to name state actors beyond Russia and China.

campaigns.operation_name: Russia Multi-Platform CIB (Africa, Ukraine, Moldova, Armenia)
campaigns.target: Africa, Ukraine, Moldova, Armenia, Western Europe
campaigns.summary: Meta H1 2026 report identified Russia as one of three leading sources of foreign influence globally with increasing sophistication. Google TAG Q4 2025 bulletin documented termination of hundreds of YouTube channels linked to Russia targeting Moldova, Armenia, and Western audiences. Portal Kombat had 57 domains blocked by Google TAG in Q1 2025. No new activity documented in May 9-15 window.
campaigns.asymmetric: Increasing use of unwitting freelance social media managers in Africa as operational cutouts represents doctrine shift toward plausible-deniability infrastructure; reduces attribution surface while maintaining operational tempo.

campaigns.target: Western audiences, US political figures, India, Tibet
campaigns.summary: Meta H1 2026 report identified China as one of three leading sources of foreign influence globally with increasing sophistication. Google TAG Q4 2025 bulletin documented ongoing terminations of China-linked YouTube channels. Microsoft MTAC has previously tracked Taizi Flood (Spamouflage) targeting US political figures. No new activity documented in May 9-15 window.
campaigns.asymmetric: High-volume spam-and-pivot model demonstrates industrial-scale account acquisition capability; small subset pivoting to political narratives suggests operational discipline prioritizing reach over immediate messaging.

actors.posture: Persistent multi-platform CIB operations targeting post-Soviet periphery (Moldova, Armenia), Western Europe, and Ukraine narratives. Increasing use of unwitting freelance social media managers in Africa as operational cutouts.
actors.doctrine_note: Portal Kombat domain network remains active across Google surfaces. Doctrine shift toward plausible-deniability infrastructure reduces attribution surface while maintaining operational tempo.
actors.headline: Russia remains most documented FIMI actor across Tier 1 sources; no new disruption disclosed this week
actors.asymmetric: Absence of new disclosure this week reflects platform reporting cadence gaps rather than operational pause; baseline activity assessed as ongoing per most recent Tier 1 material.

actors.posture: High-volume spam-and-pivot model (Spamouflage/Taizi Flood): mass account acquisition, spammy lifestyle content as cover, with small subset pivoting to political narratives targeting US-China relations, Tibet, and Taiwan.
actors.doctrine_note: Increasing sophistication noted in Meta H1 2026 report. Industrial-scale account acquisition capability demonstrates state-level resourcing.
actors.headline: China assessed as one of three leading global FIMI sources; no new platform disclosure this week
actors.asymmetric: High-volume model prioritizes reach over immediate messaging; operational discipline suggests long-term audience-building strategy rather than event-driven campaigns.

actors.posture: Iran continues cyber-enabled influence operations via IUVM and Cotton Sandstorm. Endless Mayfly attribution updated to IUVM in Meta H1 2026. New English-language operation targeting Western audiences disrupted in Q4 2025 before audience-building phase.
actors.doctrine_note: IRGC EU terrorist designation (2023) has not visibly degraded operational tempo. IUVM attribution represents increasing platform willingness to name Iranian state entities.
actors.headline: Iran IUVM attribution confirmed in Meta H1 2026; no new disruption this week
actors.asymmetric: Structural OSINT gap on IRGC-linked operations outside Meta/Google visibility; absence of disclosure does not equal absence of activity.

actors.posture: Structural OSINT gap: Gulf-state FIMI operations underrepresented in Tier 1-3 disclosures due to commercial relationships between Gulf states and Western platform infrastructure, and limited civil-society monitoring capacity in region.
actors.doctrine_note: No new disclosure found this week. Absence of disclosure does not equal absence of activity; lower confidence is structurally correct, not analytical failure.
actors.headline: No new Gulf-state FIMI disclosure found this week; structural coverage gap persists
actors.asymmetric: Commercial relationships between Gulf states and Western platforms create structural disincentive for platform-level attribution; civil-society monitoring capacity in region remains limited.

actors.posture: US-origin influence operations structurally underreported in Tier 1-3 sources due to domestic legal constraints on attribution, platform reluctance to designate US-origin CIB, and GEC reduced public output posture in 2025-2026.
actors.doctrine_note: No new disclosure found this week. Structural coverage gap reflects institutional perimeter by political design rather than evidentiary threshold.
actors.headline: No new US-origin FIMI disclosure found this week; structural coverage gap persists
actors.asymmetric: EEAS FIMI Explorer does not record US operations by documented political design; absence from institutional frameworks does not equal absence of activity. EP PEGA Committee (2022-2023) documented operations meeting DISARM TTP criteria not entered into FIMI Explorer.

actors.posture: Israeli-origin influence operations structurally underreported in Tier 1-3 sources due to platform reluctance to designate Israeli-origin CIB and absence from EEAS institutional perimeter by political design.
actors.doctrine_note: No new disclosure found this week. Structural coverage gap reflects institutional perimeter by political design rather than evidentiary threshold.
actors.headline: No new Israeli-origin FIMI disclosure found this week; structural coverage gap persists
actors.asymmetric: EEAS FIMI Explorer does not record Israeli operations by documented political design; absence from institutional frameworks does not equal absence of activity. EP PEGA Committee (2022-2023) documented operations meeting DISARM TTP criteria not entered into FIMI Explorer.

vectors.name: Platform Opacity
vectors.headline: No new Tier 1 platform disclosures in May 9-15 window; 9-15 week gaps across Meta, Google TAG, MSTIC
vectors.summary: Meta last published H1 2026 report March 11 (9 weeks ago). Google TAG last published Q4 2025 bulletin January 29 (15 weeks ago). No new MSTIC influence operations post found this week. X/Twitter structural gap persists with no CIB-equivalent mechanism.
vectors.detail: Absence of new disclosures this week reflects platform reporting cadence gaps rather than operational cessation. Meta and Google TAG typically publish quarterly; current gaps are within normal cadence but create attribution blind spots. X/Twitter structural gap (no CIB equivalent, degraded DSA coordination) remains unaddressed.
vectors.asymmetric: Platform disclosure cadence gaps create structural attribution lag; operations documented in March 2026 may have evolved significantly by May 2026 with no public visibility.

vectors.name: Attribution Gap
vectors.headline: Structural six-actor parity gap persists; Gulf, US, IL operations underrepresented in Tier 1-3 sources
vectors.summary: Gulf-state, US-origin, and Israeli-origin FIMI operations remain structurally underreported due to commercial relationships, domestic legal constraints, and EEAS institutional perimeter by political design. Lower confidence for these actors is structurally correct, not analytical failure.
vectors.detail: EEAS FIMI Explorer does not record US or Israeli operations by documented political design. EP PEGA Committee (2022-2023) documented operations meeting DISARM TTP criteria not entered into FIMI Explorer. Gulf-state operations underrepresented due to commercial relationships between Gulf states and Western platform infrastructure and limited civil-society monitoring capacity in region.
vectors.asymmetric: Six-actor parity gap creates asymmetric accountability: Russia, China, Iran operations documented at high confidence while Gulf, US, IL operations remain in monitoring status despite comparable operational scale.

vectors.name: Narrative Persistence
vectors.headline: No new narrative persistence documented this week; baseline campaigns (Doppelganger, Portal Kombat, Spamouflage) assessed as ongoing
vectors.summary: Baseline campaigns documented in Meta H1 2026 and Google TAG Q4 2025 assessed as ongoing despite absence of new disclosure. No new coordinated messaging across 3+ weeks documented in May 9-15 window.
vectors.detail: Doppelganger (RU), Portal Kombat (RU), and Spamouflage (CN) campaigns documented in prior Tier 1 disclosures assessed as ongoing per reasoner analysis. No new narrative persistence patterns documented this week.
vectors.asymmetric: Absence of new disclosure does not equal absence of narrative persistence; platform reporting cadence gaps obscure ongoing coordinated messaging.

vectors.summary: No new bot-network scale, amplification reach, or coordinated amplification data documented in May 9-15 window. Most recent data from Meta H1 2026 and Google TAG Q4 2025.
vectors.detail: Baseline amplification networks documented in prior Tier 1 disclosures assessed as ongoing. No new data on reach, scale, or coordination documented this week.
vectors.asymmetric: Platform disclosure gaps obscure current amplification reach; operations documented in March 2026 may have scaled significantly by May 2026 with no public visibility.

vectors.summary: No new synthetic media, AI-generated content, or AI-assisted FIMI infrastructure documented in May 9-15 window. Reasoner analysis notes RU actor tracker references AI-assisted content production in multi-platform operations.
vectors.detail: Reasoner analysis flags RU actor tracker noting AI-assisted content production in multi-platform operations (Doppelganger/IRA-linked); doctrine emphasizes persistence. No new Tier 1-3 disclosure on AI capability deployment this week.
vectors.asymmetric: AI-enabled FIMI capability trajectory obscured by platform disclosure gaps; doctrine development may be advancing without public visibility.

vectors.name: Regulatory Enforcement Gap
vectors.headline: No new DSA enforcement actions documented this week; X/Twitter structural gap persists
vectors.summary: No new DSA Article 40 FIMI provisions enforcement actions documented in May 9-15 window. X/Twitter structural gap (no CIB equivalent, degraded DSA coordination) remains unaddressed.
vectors.detail: EU DSA FIMI provisions in force but no new enforcement actions documented this week. X/Twitter structural gap persists with no CIB-equivalent disclosure mechanism and degraded DSA coordination. Platform disclosure cadence gaps create enforcement blind spots.
vectors.asymmetric: Regulatory enforcement gap creates accountability asymmetry: platforms with robust disclosure mechanisms (Meta, Google) face higher scrutiny than platforms with structural gaps (X/Twitter).

module_9.friction_analysis: Regulatory friction is highest at the platform disclosure layer this week. No new Tier 1 disclosures from Meta (9 weeks), Google TAG (15 weeks), or MSTIC create enforcement blind spots. X/Twitter structural gap (no CIB equivalent) remains unaddressed despite DSA obligations. EU DSA FIMI provisions are in force but enforcement actions are not publicly documented at cadence matching operational tempo.
module_9.dsa_fimi_status: EU DSA Article 40 FIMI provisions are in force. Very Large Online Platforms (VLOPs) are obligated to assess and mitigate systemic risks from coordinated inauthentic behaviour and foreign information manipulation. No new enforcement actions, fines, or Commission guidance documented in May 9-15 window. Critical path runs through platform disclosure cadence: enforcement depends on platform transparency reports, which are published quarterly at best. X/Twitter structural gap (no CIB equivalent, degraded DSA coordination) creates enforcement blind spot.

governance_gaps.analysis: Meta last published H1 2026 report March 11 (9 weeks ago). Google TAG last published Q4 2025 bulletin January 29 (15 weeks ago). No new MSTIC influence operations post found this week. Quarterly disclosure cadence creates 9-15 week attribution blind spots.
governance_gaps.asymmetric: Governance frameworks depend on platform transparency reports for enforcement triggers; quarterly cadence means operations documented in March 2026 may have evolved significantly by May 2026 with no public visibility or regulatory action.

governance_gaps.analysis: Gulf-state, US-origin, and Israeli-origin FIMI operations remain structurally underreported in Tier 1-3 sources due to commercial relationships, domestic legal constraints, and EEAS institutional perimeter by political design. Lower confidence for these actors is structurally correct, not analytical failure.
governance_gaps.asymmetric: Asymmetric accountability creates governance blind spot: Russia, China, Iran operations documented at high confidence while Gulf, US, IL operations remain in monitoring status despite comparable operational scale. EEAS FIMI Explorer does not record US or Israeli operations by documented political design.
